sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The board changed (board_changed: true) but the images look identical. Let me check what actually changed - maybe the G frame moved. Let me compare the before and after frames to see the difference.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Board changed but images look same. Let me find the actual difference
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

before_seg = before.segmentation
after_seg = after.segmentation

# Compare G frames
g_before = [n for n in before_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G']
g_after = [n for n in after_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G']

print(f"G before: {len(g_before)}, G after: {len(g_after)}")
for g in g_before:
    b = g['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"  Before: center=({(min(rows)+max(rows))//2}, {(min(cols)+max(cols))//2}), pixels={g['pixels']}")
for g in g_after:
    b = g['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"  After: center=({(min(rows)+max(rows))//2}, {(min(cols)+max(cols))//2}), pixels={g['pixels']}")

# Check all node differences
print("\nAll node colors before:", [n['color'] for n in before_seg['nodes']])
print("All node colors after:", [n['color'] for n in after_seg['nodes']])

# Check for any color changes
before_colors = sorted([(n['color'], n['hash'], n['pixels']) for n in before_seg['nodes']])
after_colors = sorted([(n['color'], n['hash'], n['pixels']) for n in after_seg['nodes']])
print(f"\nColor tuples match: {before_colors == after_colors}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Color tuples don't match but colors look same - check hashes and boundaries
before_seg = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after_seg = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation

for i, (bn, an) in enumerate(zip(before_seg['nodes'], after_seg['nodes'])):
    if bn['hash'] != an['hash'] or bn['pixels'] != an['pixels'] or bn['boundary'] != an['boundary']:
        print(f"Node {i}: color={bn['color']}->{an['color']}, hash={bn['hash']}->{an['hash']}, pixels={bn['pixels']}->{an['pixels']}")
        if bn['boundary'] != an['boundary']:
            print(f"  boundary changed")

# Also check if node counts differ
print(f"\nNode count: {len(before_seg['nodes'])} -> {len(after_seg['nodes'])}")

# Let me look at the green crosses specifically
print("\nGreen (N) nodes:")
for n in after_seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  center=({(min(rows)+max(rows))//2}, {(min(cols)+max(cols))//2}), pixels={n['pixels']}, hash={n['hash']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
